note: HOD (Head of Department) API endpoints. HOD is a distinct role on the users table (role=hod), backed by a row in the teachers table (same as a regular teacher), with teachers.department_id REQUIRED for HOD (unlike teacher, where it's optional). A department's official HOD is whoever departments.hod_teacher_id points to; Teacher::is_hod is just a computed boolean (true if Department::where('hod_teacher_id', $this->id)->exists()). HOD has its own dashboard scoped to their department, and is auto-scoped on the shared /api/sessions endpoints to only see sessions within their department. HOD CANNOT start/end sessions or block/unblock students — those remain teacher-only actions.

1_register_hod.request_notes: department_id IS required for role=hod (unlike role=teacher, where it's optional). This is enforced in RegisterRequest::rules() — 'hod' => required|integer|exists:departments,id. employee_no must also be unique in the teachers table.

body.message: The department id field is required.

department_id: The department id field is required.

body.message: The selected department id is invalid.

department_id: The selected department id is invalid.

body.message: The employee no has already been taken.

employee_no: The employee no has already been taken.

body.message: Your account is inactive. Please contact the administrator.

3_hod_dashboard.description: HOD-only route (middleware role:hod). Returns department info plus department-wide aggregate counts and today's attendance percentage across the whole department (DashboardService::hod()).

notes: 'department.hod' is only populated when the relation is eager-loaded with 'hod' — here it reflects whoever departments.hod_teacher_id points to, which may or may not be the currently logged-in HOD if multiple teachers exist in the department.

'department.teachers_count' / 'department.students_count' inside the nested DepartmentResource are null here because those are whenCounted() fields not loaded by this endpoint — use the top-level teachers_count/students_count instead.
today_sessions_count counts ALL active+ended sessions today across every batch/program under this department, not just one teacher's classes.
today_attendance_percentage = round(present_attendance_rows / total_attendance_rows * 100, 1) across all of today's sessions in the department; 0.0 if no sessions ran today.

'programs' is the NEW per-program breakdown — one entry per Program row under this department (regardless of whether it had any classes today), each with its own today_sessions_count and today_attendance_percentage computed the same way as the department-level totals but filtered to that program's batches. A program with zero sessions today shows today_sessions_count: 0 and today_attendance_percentage: 0.0 — same convention as the department-level fields.

body.message: No department is assigned to this HOD account.

error_response_no_department.note: Thrown when the HOD's teacher row has no department_id set (or no teacher row exists at all).

body.message: You do not have permission to perform this action.

error_response_wrong_role.note: Returned by the role:hod middleware if a non-HOD account (e.g. role=teacher) calls this endpoint.

4_sessions_list_department_scoped.description: Shared endpoint for every role, but auto-scoped per role in SessionController::index(). For HOD it returns every ClassSession whose timetable->batch->program->department_id matches the HOD's own teacher->department_id — i.e. ALL sessions run by ALL teachers in their department, not just their own.

success_response.known_gap: Paginated server-side (15 per page) but pagination meta (current_page/last_page/total) is not included in the response — only the flat array in 'data'.

5_session_show_department_scoped.description: Authorized via ClassSessionPolicy::view(). HOD may view any session whose timetable->batch->program->department_id equals their own teacher->department_id (i.e. any session within their department, regardless of which teacher ran it). Includes the full per-session present/absent/student counts (requires 'attendances' to be loaded, which this action does).

full TimetableResource: batch, course, teacher, room, time_slot

error_response_outside_department.note: Happens when the session belongs to a timetable/batch/program in a department other than the HOD's own.

6_actions_hod_cannot_perform.description: These endpoints exist but are gated to role=teacher only, either by route middleware (role:teacher group) or by policy. An HOD token calling them gets rejected before reaching any business logic.

body.message: You do not have permission to perform this action.
You do not have permission to perform this action.

session_attendance_detail.note: Even though HOD can see a session's present/absent/student COUNTS via 5_session_show_department_scoped, the full per-student attendance breakdown endpoint is teacher-only.

body.message: You do not have permission to perform this action.

admin_management_endpoints.note: HOD has no write access to teachers, timetables, departments, programs, batches, rooms, or students — those remain admin-only in the current implementation. HOD is read-only/aggregate-only (dashboard + department-scoped session viewing).

body.message: You do not have permission to perform this action.

success_response.note: HOD only receives notifications generated for their own account; there is no department-wide notification feed.

10_logout_shared.note: Only revokes the current device's token; other logged-in devices for the same HOD stay logged in.

11_get_authenticated_user_shared.note: Best endpoint to call after login/app-resume to confirm current role/status — always reflects live DB state. role will read 'hod' here, never a separate flag.

12_data_model_reference.note: How HOD is identified and distinguished from a plain teacher, for anyone building the frontend or writing tests.
12_data_model_reference.users_table: role column is an enum including 'hod' — this is the actual source of truth checked by route middleware (role:hod) and User::isHod().
12_data_model_reference.teachers_table: Every HOD also has a row here (same table as teachers), with department_id REQUIRED and non-null for HOD accounts (enforced at registration, not by a DB constraint).
12_data_model_reference.departments_table: hod_teacher_id (nullable FK to teachers.id) marks which single teacher is the official head of that department. This is set by an admin (e.g. via PUT /api/departments/{id}) — registering with role=hod does NOT automatically set this column.
12_data_model_reference.teacher_is_hod_computed_attribute: Teacher::is_hod is a computed boolean: Department::where('hod_teacher_id', $this->id)->exists(). It reflects whether THIS SPECIFIC teacher row is the official department head — it has nothing to do with users.role. In theory a user with role=hod might not be set as hod_teacher_id on any department yet (is_hod would be false), and conversely a plain role=teacher row could theoretically be pointed to by hod_teacher_id.
12_data_model_reference.dashboard_scoping_uses_role_not_flag: DashboardService::hod() and SessionController's HOD branch both scope by $user->teacher->department_id (the HOD's own home department from the teachers table), NOT by checking is_hod or hod_teacher_id. So an HOD only ever sees their own department's data, determined purely by their teacher row's department_id.
